assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 49-55 show the Acceptance Criteria Detail table with all 5 criteria marked PASS: single license filter (criterion-1.md), comma-separated filter (criterion-2.md), invalid license returns 400 (criterion-3.md), filter integrates with pagination (criterion-4.md), response shape unchanged (criterion-5.md). Each criterion-*.md file has 'Verdict: PASS'.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR files match task specification exactly: 2 modified files and 1 new file, all accounted for'. Lines 24: 'Scope Containment: PASS -- PR modifies exactly the files specified in the task: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs and modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (Files to Modify), and creates tests/api/package.rs (Files to Create). No out-of-scope files, no unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The output consists entirely of report files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) which are verification/analysis documents. No output file contains any code modifications, git commits, file edits, or write operations to code files. The report only references reading and analyzing code (e.g., criterion-1.md: 'The PackageListParams struct gains a new optional field' describes what the diff contains, not what the skill did).

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 17: 'Overall: PASS' followed by a summary of findings. The report concludes with analysis and evidence but contains no merge commands, no 'gh pr merge', and no indication of automatic merging. The report structure (tables, domain sub-agent summaries, criterion details) is clearly a presentation of findings for human review.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines'. Lines 28-29: 'Sensitive Pattern Scan: PASS -- Scanned all added lines across 3 files. No hardcoded passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud credentials, or database credentials detected. The diff contains only Rust code (imports, struct fields, validation logic, query builder code, and test functions).'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Only new test file added (tests/api/package.rs with 4 test functions); no modified or deleted tests'. Lines 44-45: 'Test Change Classification: ADDITIVE -- tests/api/package.rs is a new file with 4 new test functions and associated assertions. No test files were modified or deleted.'

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 23-45 contain 'Domain Sub-Agent Summary' with four sections: 'Intent Alignment' (Scope Containment: PASS, Diff Size: PASS, Commit Traceability: PASS), 'Security' (Sensitive Pattern Scan: PASS), 'Correctness' (CI Status: PASS, Acceptance Criteria: PASS, Verification Commands: N/A), 'Style/Conventions' (Convention Upgrade: N/A, Repetitive Test Detection: PASS, Test Documentation: PASS, Test Change Classification: ADDITIVE). All specified verdicts are present under the correct domain sub-agents.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (report.md line 24): file-by-file scope comparison listing 'modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs and modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (Files to Modify), and creates tests/api/package.rs (Files to Create)'. Security (line 29): line-level detail 'Scanned all added lines across 3 files. No hardcoded passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys...'. Correctness (lines 33-39): per-criterion code-level verification for all 5 acceptance criteria with specific code references (e.g., 'validate_license_param parses the value', 'PackageService::list applies is_in filter', 'spdx::Expression::parse validation with AppError::BadRequest'). Plus criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive code-level evidence with code snippets. Style/Conventions (lines 43-45): test quality assessment noting '4 test functions each test distinct behaviors' and 'All 4 test functions have /// doc comments'.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No reviews or comments exist on the PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. These appear in the main summary table (lines 4-16) but are NOT listed under any of the four domain sub-agent sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions in lines 23-45), confirming they are determined independently by the orchestrator.
